title: Fix the Inefficient JavaScript State Reducer

statement.markdown: Implement a function that reduces a state object by applying a given reducer function to each state. However, the current implementation has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to the use of Array.forEach(). Optimize the function to achieve a time complexity of O(n) by utilizing a more efficient data structure.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes an object representing the current state and a function that takes state and action as arguments.
statement.notes: The reducer function is expected to return a new state object. Do not modify the original state object.

constraints.description: The number of properties in the state object

examples.explanation: The reducer function increments the value of 'a' when the action is 'INCREMENT'.
The reducer function decrements the value of 'z' when the action is 'DECREMENT'.

hints.text: Use a more efficient data structure to store the state properties.
Avoid using Array.forEach() and instead use a more efficient method to iterate over the state properties.

editorial.title: Optimizing the State Reducer
editorial.intuition: The current implementation uses Array.forEach(), which has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to the creation of a new array for each iteration. To optimize this, we can use a Map to store the state properties, which allows for O(1) lookup and insertion.
editorial.bruteForce: The current implementation uses Array.forEach() to iterate over the state properties, resulting in a time complexity of O(n^2).
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a Map to store the state properties, which allows for O(1) lookup and insertion.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the 'reduce()' method to iterate over the state properties, which has a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) because it only requires a single pass over the state properties.
